title: Volcanic Ash Aviation Hazard Detection And Airspace Management Gaps
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping volcanic ash detection capability and ash-concentration forecast accuracy along aviation routes, with explicit satellite detection limitations, eruption-source parameter uncertainty, and regional monitoring-network coverage assessment.
why_it_matters: Volcanic ash causes engine failure, windshield abrasion, and flight-system contamination. The 2010 Eyjafjallajökull eruption caused over 100,000 flight cancellations and $5 billion in economic losses. ICAO's nine VAACs provide global monitoring, but detection capability varies dramatically — the East African Rift, Central Asian volcanic arcs, and Southeast Asian volcanic belts have far sparser monitoring than the North Atlantic and Pacific. Without verified ash-detection capability assessment, aviation risk management in poorly monitored volcanic regions relies on assumptions that may not hold.

success_metrics.target: Every ash-detection capability claim links to satellite product accuracy, ground-monitor density, and VAAC coverage data with documented regional gaps.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against VAAC coverage and satellite detection documentation.

success_metrics.target: No ash-concentration forecast claim is merged without documenting eruption-source parameter uncertainty propagation and model validation status.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms forecast uncertainty methodology and eruption-source parameter documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every detection-gap analysis documents the risk of missed detections in poorly monitored volcanic regions, including submarine volcanoes and remote volcanic arcs.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms false-negative risk documentation for every detection-gap claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, aviation-safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
